commonIssues:
Discrepancies in spelling, initials, or surname formats across Aadhaar cards, PAN cards, passports, and school certificates are the leading cause of delays.

How to fix:

  • Update your Aadhaar or PAN card to match your official school certificates before applying.

Users often report problems uploading documents, server downtime, or issues with payment processing on the official Delhi government website.

How to fix:

  • Use the e-District Delhi Portal during off-peak hours and ensure your browser allows pop-ups for payment gateways.

Applying at the wrong Sub-Divisional Magistrate (SDM) office, when the applicant does not reside in that specific district for the required 30 days, leads to immediate rejection or long delays.

How to fix:

  • Apply only at the SDM office of the district where either partner has resided for at least 30 days.

Incomplete or improperly drafted affidavits regarding marital status or date of marriage can trigger verification objections.

How to fix:

  • Download the standardized affidavit format from the e-District portal and get it notarized correctly.

Lack of proper documentation for a marriage that has already taken place (e.g., lack of wedding photos showing rituals like varmala or saptapadi), especially if no priest certificate is available.

How to fix:

  • Submit an affidavit, wedding invitation, and multiple photographs showing the couple performing rituals.

Difficulty in having two, or sometimes three (for Special Marriage Act), witnesses with proper identification and address proof present on the appointment day.

How to fix:

  • Coordinate with witnesses well in advance. They must bring original Aadhaar and PAN cards on the appointment date.

While the target timeline is often quoted as 14–30 days, physical verification of documents can significantly extend this period, leading to frustration, particularly when urgent certificates are needed for visas or passports.

How to fix:

  • Track status online. If delayed beyond 30 days, file a grievance on the portal or visit the SDM office.

Despite the online process, a mandatory in-person visit to the SDM office with witnesses is required, which can be challenging to coordinate.

How to fix:

  • Arrive at the SDM office 15 minutes before your scheduled appointment time with all required persons.

Typographical errors or wrong entries made by the registry office, such as misspelled names, wrong dates, or incorrect parent details, which require filing for correction.

How to fix:

  • Submit a written application for correction to the SDM along with proof of correct details.

For marriages under the Special Marriage Act (1954), the required 30-day public notice period cannot be bypassed, leading to long waits for inter-religious couples.

How to fix:

  • The 30-day notice is legally mandatory under SMA. Plan your registration timeline accordingly.

While a 1-3 day Tatkal service exists, its availability or application is sometimes inconsistently managed across different district offices.

How to fix:

  • Inquire directly at the SDM office regarding Tatkal availability and ensure all documents are flawlessly prepared.
